narration: Initialize userScore and cpuScore variables to 0.
Create an infinite while loop (while true) to handle game rounds.
Prompt user to enter 1 for rock, 2 for paper, 3 for scissors.
Import java.util.Scanner and create a Scanner object named scan.
Read user input using scan.nextInt() and store in userChoice.
Import java.util.Random and create a Random object named rand.
Generate random CPU choice using rand.nextInt(3) + 1.
Use if-else if statements to handle all game scenarios.
Check for ties: if cpuChoice equals userChoice, display tie message.
If user picks 1 (rock) and CPU picks 2 (paper), user loses round.
If user picks 1 (rock) and CPU picks 3 (scissors), user wins round.
If user picks 2 (paper) and CPU picks 3 (scissors), user loses round.
If user picks 3 (scissors) and CPU picks 1 (rock), user wins round.
After each round, update scores and display current scores using printf.
Check if either score reaches 2 to break the infinite loop.
If user wins best two out of three, display congratulations message.
If CPU wins best two out of three, display loss message.
Ensure all brackets are properly closed.
